text: Solar panels convert sunlight into electricity using the photovoltaic effect. When sunlight hits the panel, photons (light particles) knock electrons free from their atoms. These free electrons are guided by an electric field to flow in a circuit, creating usable electrical current. ReVision Energy +3This process occurs in four primary stages: 1. Absorption of Sunlight Most solar panels are made of silicon, a semiconductor material. Solar cells consist of two specially treated layers—one with a positive charge and one with a negative charge. When sunlight strikes the solar panel, the cells absorb the sun's photons. Iberdrola +32. Creation of Electric Current The energy from the absorbed photons excites the silicon atoms. This energy is strong enough to knock electrons free from their atoms, leaving a "hole" behind. Because the solar cell has positive and negative layers, an internal electric field is created. This field acts as a magnet, pushing the freed electrons to the top of the cell and directing the holes to the bottom. YouTube·The Engineering Mindset +13. Capturing the Electricity Metal conductive plates (often visible as fine grid lines on the panel) collect the moving electrons. Once collected, these electrons flow through external wires, creating a Direct Current (DC) electrical circuit. Switch Together +14. Conversion and Usage Because standard home appliances and the electrical grid run on Alternating Current (AC), the DC electricity must first pass through an inverter. The inverter transforms the DC power into usable AC power. This electricity then either powers your home, charges a battery, or is sent back to the electrical grid. Maxeon Solar Technologies +1For a visual step-by-step breakdown of how these components work together in a residential setting:8:03How Home Solar Systems Work: A Beginner's GuideSolarQuotesYouTube• 21 lug 2025
